description: Question bank for evaluating GraphRAG answer quality + spoiler safety. Each question has a reader cursor (max_chapter) and expected ground-truth metadata.

questions.question: Who is Marley and what is his relationship to Scrooge?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Jacob Marley is Scrooge's deceased business partner. His ghost visits Scrooge on Christmas Eve to warn him and announce that three more spirits will come.

questions.question: What kind of person is Scrooge at the start of the book?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Scrooge is a cold, miserly, and solitary businessman who hates Christmas and treats others with contempt.

questions.question: Who is Bob Cratchit?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Bob Cratchit is Scrooge's underpaid clerk who struggles to support his family, including his ill son Tiny Tim.

questions.question: What does Fred invite Scrooge to?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Fred, Scrooge's cheerful nephew, invites Scrooge to Christmas dinner, which Scrooge refuses.

questions.question: Why does Marley's ghost wear chains?
questions.expected_answer_gist: The chains represent the selfish deeds and cash-boxes he forged in life; he wears them as punishment and warns Scrooge to change his ways.

questions.question: Who is Tiny Tim?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Tiny Tim is Bob Cratchit's youngest son. He is ill and represents innocence and the spirit of Christmas.

questions.question: Who is the last spirit that visits Scrooge, and what does the spirit show him?
questions.expected_answer_gist: The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come (the Last of the Spirits) shows Scrooge scenes of his own lonely death and neglected grave, prompting his vow to change.

questions.question: What does Scrooge see on the bed that horrifies him?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Scrooge sees a man who has died alone and unloved lying on the bed; he realizes the figure is himself in the future.

questions.question: How does Scrooge change by the end of the book?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Scrooge undergoes a complete transformation into a generous, kind man who keeps Christmas well. He raises Bob Cratchit's salary and becomes a second father to Tiny Tim.

questions.question: What is the central theme of A Christmas Carol?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Redemption through compassion: even a hardened miser can be transformed by confronting his past, present, and future and by embracing generosity and the spirit of Christmas.

questions.question: What gift does Scrooge send to the Cratchits?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Scrooge anonymously sends a large prize turkey to Bob Cratchit's house on Christmas Day.

questions.question: Does Tiny Tim die?
questions.expected_answer_gist: Based on what the reader has seen so far (through chapter 1), Tiny Tim is ill but his fate is not yet resolved. The answer should NOT reveal future-chapter outcomes. This is a spoiler-trap question.
